vulnerability.title: Enable Certmap in SSSD
vulnerability.description: SSSD should be configured to verify the certificate of the user or group. To set this up ensure that section like certmap/testing.test/rule_name is setup in /etc/sssd/sssd.conf. For example [certmap/testing.test/rule_name] matchrule =<SAN>.*EDIPI@mil maprule = (userCertificate;binary={cert!bin}) domains = testing.test
vulnerability.recommendation: Review and remediate: Enable Certmap in SSSD

triage_decision.reason: The finding requires adding a certmap stanza to /etc/sssd/sssd.conf and restarting the SSSD service. This is a standard authentication configuration change with minimal risk of system breakage or lockout when performed correctly.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; filesystems affected; potential authentication misconfiguration

review_feedback: The plan edits /etc/sssd/sssd.conf and restarts SSSD without creating a backup or performing a syntax check. A malformed stanza could prevent SSSD from starting, which would break authentication for services that rely on it (e.g., SSH, PAM), potentially locking out users.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The plan will add the required certmap stanza and restart SSSD, which should resolve the vulnerability. However, it lacks safety checks such as backing up the original configuration, validating the syntax before restart, and using a reload instead of a full restart to minimize service disruption.
concerns: No backup of /etc/sssd/sssd.conf before overwriting could lead to loss of original configuration if an error occurs.
The plan does not validate the new configuration syntax (e.g., using sssctl config-check) before restarting the service, which could cause SSSD to fail to start.
Inserting the stanza after the [sssd] section may not be the conventional location; certmap sections are usually placed after the relevant domain section.
Using a full restart may cause temporary authentication outages; a reload could be safer.
suggested_improvements: Create a backup of the original sssd.conf (e.g., cp /etc/sssd/sssd.conf /etc/sssd/sssd.conf.bak) before making changes.
Validate the updated configuration with `sssd -t` or `sssd -d 2` after editing and before restarting.
Place the new certmap stanza after the appropriate [domain/...] section rather than after [sssd] to follow typical ordering.
Consider using `systemctl reload sssd` instead of a full restart to avoid disrupting active sessions.
Add a step to check the service status after reload/restart and verify that authentication works (e.g., test a login).
